reviews.review_text: Came for 2 nights, stayed almost 5 weeks, what a great location, go to sleep to the sound of the waves, km's of beaches for walking on, up to the lighthouse, they sound the Fog Horn every Sunday at 12, walk to Georgetown for a coffee, only takes an hour, kayak on the river, yes the facilities are a bit dated but are always clean, its quiet, peaceful and lots of local wildlife, shelterd also as Low Head can get windy, we will return.

We became stranded in low head due to our boat breaking down. Had not had a shower for a week (tired stressed dirty). Walked 20mins to this caravan park and asked if we could pay some money to use the hot showers. The female manager very rude, denied our request and told us to use cold outdoor shower at the beach. It was 15 degrees with an icy wind. We have traveled all around Australia and have never been treated this badly. Big 4 up the road happy to help, lovely manager. I would have given no stars if it were an option :(
One of the sweetest and cleanest camper park. Dark night, full of stars, close to penguin site, and I'm pretty sure we heard a Tassie devil at night. So cool!

I stayed in a pet friendly cabin on arrival in was told the cabins water had recently been fixed just to turn the hot on first then the cold when I tried it I only had a dribble and luke warm water I told the caretaker who said there was nothing she could do and to shower in my sister's  cabin who was also staying there on top off that the cabin was filthy when I told her about the dead spiders on the floor dust and cobwebs she said it was cleaned the day before as well some of my family were staying at powered sites in caravans at 9.30 pm she went around with a torch told everyone to be quite when all they were doing was sitting talking she also knocked on my cousins cabin door and told them to be quiet they were only talking this was not just us it was everyone in caravan park who was still sitting outside talking this is a poorly run park and rude caretaker's
Beautiful beach Park in a bush setting, quiet and full of wildlife 😁

Lets be clear, this isn't a 5 star hotel, it's a basic caravan park, but the location and facilities are what can be expected. The location in particular is great. Add the fact that profits go to supplying wheelchairs the those in need, it's 5 stars in my book.

Terrible Woman on counter. $15 to Self contain camp between the road and fence, but to use the toilet (amenities) $40- Thats an expensive loo break & amenities have been let go and are poor. Whole place was untidy
not good - the cabins are in need of a revamp and a nice deep clean 🧽

We were thinking about booking a cabin as we were doing the night penguin tour nearby. We ended up just staying at an unpowered in the rooftop and thank god we did, the cabins were priced at $150 per night and they were extremely run down, I can’t imagine what the insides looked like. The bathrooms were dirty, there were dead spiders and insect faeces on the window sills and spiders webs covering the ceiling. There was only one other person staying at an unpowered site and instead of spacing them out to the numerous other spaces, they plonked them directly next to us so we could smell his cigarette smoke and hear every fart. The showers were hot and good pressure, which was about the only good thing about the place.

The cabin was disgusting don’t  know how that passed any sort of inspection to be rented at $150 per night rotting filthy curtains stained carpet mould in shower smelly in the cabin the actual caravan park amenities for campers did appear to be ok location is great our issue was our cabin it was disgusting we stayed as we were with other family who had caravan we spent our time at their site rather than cabin

Definitely only good things to say about this place ,we arrived new years day to a full park but when we ask the two ladies at reception for a private camp site they were more than happy to accommodate us and put us in a lovely little spot called Helens site, we had a very relaxing time and found all staff to be polite and helpful ,all amenities were lovely and clean ,and we will definitely be back
New CAFÉ operators are doing a fairly good job.Certainly cheap enough...

I have stayed here multiple times in the past & its been great! Thats ehy we decided to come back BUT!!! The new office manager, I think her name may have been Anneka. Is the rudest, most arrogant person in any caravan park I have stayed in.... (& trust me, that is a long list). She hasn't even got any general knowledge of the local area! 🤣😕
The park is neglected & not very well maintained.... so much for a Grounds Manager, the only time you see him is if he's collecting rubbish in the morning.
We stayed for 3 nights on a powered site. There was ample space but she booked us in a site beside another caravan.....🤔 why not space people out, if there is room to do so!
The location is beautiful but I wouldn't stay here again! If you were thinking of staying at Low Head, I would recommend the Big4!
It's dated but hey...who cares. Were self contained... theyre working on it...updating but staff were friendly and helpful..
Beautiful overnight stay and the tree carvings were amazing
Great little place with a Rustic feel, close to the beach to view the sun setting behind the lighthouse. 100% better then any Big4.

We stayed 2 nights in an unpowered tent site. Check in was easy and quick. The staff were all friendly and the owner/manager walked us to our site. We were tucked away in a quiet corner out of the wind. The bush around our camp site was the perfect spot to view the wildlife, we saw many wallabies. The camp kitchen was great. Fully stocked with all you will need from toaster and kettle to fridge and oven. The bathrooms were clean and tidy. It was only a very short walk over the dune to the beach, with stunning views of the lighthouse.

Great spot we camped the night nice grassy areas, they allow fire pits and staff are fantastic.
Beach is close just a short walk I highly recommend this spot.

We arrived 3 hours after the office closed, but were warmly welcomed by the owners who opened up just for us to take payment. They then gave us a nighttime tour of the campsite and took the time to show us the best spot to pitch our tent, sheltered from the wind. The setting was ideal, with the sound of the waves during the night and surrounded by a colony of pademelons.
The shared facilities, including the toilets and kitchen, were very clean.
A very pleasant stay !

My wife and I stayed 2 nights in our 8m motorhome on a powered site on a Wednesday and Thursday early March.
Our site was right next to some bush and very close to toilets, showers, and camp kitchen. The gentleman who checked us in was friendly and informative without going on forever about what to do in the area.
There were only a few other caravans and a motorhome near us and it was very quiet with the sounds of the ocean from over the nearby sand dunes.
The facilities however, desperately need an update as they are old, with lots of broken items and the soap dispensers in the toilet area were empty in the ladies.
There are two men's showers and one had a broken off door latch just sitting there.
We did like the area and bike riding into Georgetown from there and enjoyed our two days.

Super friendly managers and extremely helpful. Great level sites and fairly sheltered. Beautiful beach but often windy. Clean bathroom facilities with free hot water showers. Good kitchen facilities. Fire pots provided. Will definitely stay again.

We stayed at the powered campsite close to the beach which is just about 2-3 minutes walk. It’s really nice place  with clean toilets and showers. Kim was amazing and helpful to us. If we coming back then here! Also a reason to stay here was the penguins night tour. Can recommend it a lot, is just 5 minutes drive or 20 minutes walk from the campsite.
Camped/Unpowered was easy to move around, camp kitchen had microwave so convenient, bathrooms very clean... so close to beach 👍 good local info from owner on points to see.
Love this caravan park..so close to beach..very laid back..oK so not fancy but some of us dont need fancy
Love this place❤️
The best owners! Friendly and they make sure that you are comfortable. Hot showers and we had an ensuite which was great. Camp kitchen was well appointed, beach was 1min walk, highly recommend.

Friendly staff gave us some good advice about the area
Close to the penguin tour
Can walk to the beach and see some people surfing
We had power. freshwater and greywater wasted on site
Nice lovely park with cool sculptures
We had a (short but) great stay. Very kind owner that gives you the ins and outs about the surroundings and wants your stay to be as comfortable as possible
We love East Beach. Staff are always friendly, everything is neat and clean. Nothing is ever a problem. Would recommend it to everyone. Not the owners fault, but very limited phone or TV reception.

We stayed here in our caravan for 4 nights on an unpowered site.
Water available to fill tanks but not to attach to the caravan. The layout of the park is user friendly.
There is a cafe and restaurant on site.
The park is very central to travel to tourist sites.
In camper so didn't use rooms. Location great. Staff great. Wildlife everywhere. Worth a visit

We stayed on a powered site. Absolutely fantastic location, nice big sites where you walk directly onto the beach. Very friendly staff, the amenities just need a bit of an update but loved our stay here 😀
Good park.Very good host.Great location.
Great little park aged but has everything you need
Beautiful, peaceful, well maintained older style caravan park. Great Managers with very pleasant manor. Great proximity to beach & local attractions
Very relaxed. Great atmosphere. Next to the beach and places to visit and a park not far to walk. Not far from town. My kids loved it. Will come back again!

Wow great camping with the family. As soon as you drive in you get a very relaxed and good vibe about this park. Stacey and Allen great hosts. Allen walks everyone in to welcome them. We had a great time an en-suite a god send for us. The beach fabulous. Do yourself a favour and have a holiday at this park. We will be back. Thank you for making this a memorable experience.

We spent a week over Christmas/New Year here and it was amazing. Stacey and Allan are very welcoming, friendly and personable. Very easy to deal with and always very helpful. We had the best time in our Jayco Jpod. Love having the use of the camp kitchens and amenities. Everyone was very respectful of each other which made it a very pleasant experience. It's also a pet friendly park and although we didn't have a pet with us, everyone that did, certainly did the right thing and their dogs were very well behaved. We love the fact that we are so close to the beautiful low head beach ⛱️. The sunsets were absolutely stunning and so were the stars 🌟 nights. Listening to the waves rolling in of a night was very peaceful. We were blessed with great weather too which made for swimming 🏊‍♀️ everyday and walks along the beach ⛱️. We would highly recommend staying at this little slice of paradise only 45 minutes drive from Launceston. Thanks Stacey and Allan for the great service and for our stay. Chaylea and Chris
Welcoming and lovely hosts Allan & Stacey.  Well kept grounds, clean facilities.  Beaut beach & sand dune, loved our stay out in the back area.  Would certainly come back to stay.  Thankyou. 👌👍🙂
Great campsite tucked away behind the beach. The owners were so warm and welcoming, walking us to our site and letting us book after they’d closed the office. Clean facilities. Thank you so much!!
Perfect location and very friendly owners, beautiful place to stay I could live here.

We stopped in for a night as we toured the east coast. Excellent, clean toilets and laundry, lovely hot (and free!) showers. Easy access to a beautiful beach! We’ve stayed here before and will again, thank you!
